journal_entry.description: Purchase of tea, beverages, and refreshments via corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Tea and beverages for office use are recorded as supplies or office expense. The 10% tax rate suggests this is partially tax-exempt (basic groceries are tax-exempt in Canada, but prepared beverages and certain refreshments may attract HST). The tax paid is recoverable as HST Receivable. Credit card liability increases.

clarification_questions: Asset increase (Office Supplies) and liability increase (Credit Card Payable). The 10% tax is treated as recoverable HST (ON rate is 13%, but the effective 10% suggests a mix of taxable and tax-exempt items, or the stated rate in the transaction). HST Receivable is recorded as an additional debit line for the tax component.
